⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israeli military confirmed that it struck the Marvdasht petrochemical complex near Shiraz, one of the few remaining facilities in Iran producing nitric acid for explosives and ballistic missile materials. The strike followed the bombing of Iran’s largest petrochemical plant in Asaluyeh. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The Israeli military said it struck struck another central crossing over the Litani River allegedly used by Hezbollah to move between north and south Lebanon and to transfer weapons, rockets and launchers. A total of seven crossings have been hit since the start of Operation Roaring Lion. The IDF said it will continue to act with intensity against Hezbollah. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Israeli Channel 12: The next 24 hours are the most decisive of the war, an Israeli official said. If it were up to Iran’s political leadership, a ceasefire would have been reached long ago. “There is doubt about their control,” the source added. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to ResistanceTrench mirror: "🇮🇷⚡️🇮🇱🇺🇸 Images of the American-Israeli attack on Iran’s Sharif University of Technology last night." 🇮🇷 Missile strikes failed to stop classes at Sharif University.

🇺🇳 ⚡️ UN Security Council Set to Vote Today on Bahrain's Hormuz Resolution The UN Security Council is voting today on a resolution about the Strait of Hormuz, sponsored by Bahrain (who's currently the Council president). The original draft, pushed by Arab countries, was sought stronger language under Chapter VII of the UN Charter. This would have authorized member states to use " all necessary means " (including military force, potentially through naval coalitions). China and Russia pushed back hard, calling it escalatory, so they watered it down a lot. Now it just "strongly encourages" countries to team up on defensive stuff like escorting ships, no actual force allowed. Outcome is still uncertain. Russia or China might abstain or even veto.

⚡️ 🇮🇷 Iran’s judiciary chief, Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i, has ordered that sentences for enemy agents be issued more quickly, using existing laws including the law on intensifying punishment for espionage. He stressed the need for accuracy and justice. Citing the full‑scale war with the front of arrogance. He added that Iran is exercising its legitimate right to defence and will continue until national security is ensured. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel After being flooded by hundreds of Saudi accounts claiming this is old footage, I verified it is from yesterday. Saudi Arabia’s Jubail complex was indeed hit by two Iranian missiles. The Al Jazeera clip they are citing as well as the Reuters screenshot refers to a completely separate incident at the Khor Mor gas field in 2024. Saudi users face restrictions on posting strike-related content, so some move to the comments, claim it’s from Kurdish region, or try to push community notes. tweet

Earlier versions included language allowing…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations Amir Saeid Iravani says Donald Trump recent threats against Iran constitute direct incitement to terrorism and clear evidence of intent to commit war crimes under international law. He warns that threats to target civilian infrastructure, including power plants and bridges, pose a direct danger to civilians and that deliberately targeting such infrastructure constitutes a war crime and state terrorism. He calls on the United Nations Security Council and the UN Secretary-General to take immediate action to condemn the threats and ensure accountability, warning that inaction will undermine international law and embolden further violations. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇳 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 Reuters : The UN Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the draft has been significantly weakened after China opposed authorising the use of force. Earlier versions included language allowing “all necessary means,” but the latest draft instead focuses on defensive coordination to ensure safe navigation. @wfwitness
